title: Campaign Creator - A Great Tool for Solo RPGs, from Shieldice Studio
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=yXWhKEoL3QU

Daniel (@dungeondive) looks at the Campaign Creator deck from Shieldice Studio (makers of the Realm Fables books), a small set of cards for plotting a solo sandbox campaign before you sit down to play. It's organised into a few interlocking decks: red Quest cards form the main beats, blue Interlude cards drop in moments of respite between them, and green Theme cards attach a flavour to each quest or to the campaign as a whole — laid out in a line, quests above, themes below.

A separate set of eight Story-element cards (enemies, allies, setting, lore, problems, solutions, secrets, objectives) gives writing prompts to flesh each quest out, and individual cards carry d6 sub-tables for finer detail. Daniel deals a sample spread live and improvises a coherent urban arc — impersonating a guild official, infiltrating a brewers' guild to free a kidnapped brewmaster, an underground arena, an interdimensional-portal twist — to show how a string of draws becomes a session-zero skeleton you then expand.

He pegs it as a natural companion to Scarlet Heroes, whose in-book solo rules cover the town, wilderness and dungeon settings the deck throws at you. Another genuinely useful tool he's surprised gets so little discussion.

----

Do you plot a solo campaign in advance with tools like this, or prefer to discover the whole thing emergently at the table?
